_meta.description: Hero spec hierarchy for 4:5 Facebook listing card — 4-spec bottom strip per equipment type. Grounded in use-case scorer research (ctl_use_case_scorer_v1_0.py, skid_steer_use_case_scorer_v1_0.py, mini_ex_use_case_scorer.py), hard-gate thresholds, and hundreds of real dealer listings analyzed across MachineryTrader, EquipmentTrader, and Facebook Marketplace.
_meta.field_namespace: All primary and fallback field names match canonical registry field names per spec_display_profiles.json conventions. Fields live in the record's 'specs' object unless noted source:feature_flags or source:dealer_input.
_meta.confidence_note: require_confidence is checked against field_confidence[field_name] on the registry record. Fields sourced from feature_flags have no field_confidence entry — require_confidence NONE bypasses the gate entirely. DealerInput fields have no registry confidence and are always trusted if present.
locked_standards: CTL 2026-04-10: aux_flow_high_gpm banned from buyer-facing output. Strip uses aux_flow_standard_gpm. High-flow badge sources from DealerInput 'high_flow' boolean, not registry feature_flags.high_flow_available (suppressed).
SSL 2026-04-10: aux_flow_high_gpm IS buyer-facing for SSL (not banned). High-flow badge sources from DealerInput 'high_flow' boolean — registry feature_flags.high_flow_available suppressed.
MEX: canonical aux flow field is aux_flow_primary_gpm, not aux_flow_standard_gpm. Dump height is max_dump_height_ft in feet; CTL/SSL use dump_height_in in inches — do not cross-reference units.

MEX tail_swing_type uses short value keys — zero, conventional, reduced — consistent with CTL/SSL. Corrected 2026-04-17: prior documentation incorrectly claimed MEX used descriptive keys (zero_tail_swing etc.); direct verification of all 63 registry records confirmed short keys only.
known_field_mismatches: aux_flow_high_gpm (CTL/SSL) — not modeled in MEX registry
high_flow_available feature_flag (CTL/SSL) — not modeled in MEX registry
dump_height_in/inches (SSL) vs max_dump_height_ft/feet (MEX) — name and unit both differ
hydraulic_pressure_standard_psi (CTL/SSL) vs hydraulic_pressure_psi + aux_pressure_primary_psi (MEX — two fields, one concept, needs verification)
feature_flags fields have no field_confidence entries — confidence gate cannot be enforced for enclosed_cab_available, high_flow_available, two_speed_available

spec_strip._note: aux_flow_high_gpm is banned from buyer-facing CTL output per locked standard 2026-04-10. Standard GPM shown here; high-flow capability signaled via badge_zone only.

spec_strip.render_hint: boolean — render 'Enclosed' when true; drop cell when false or null

badges.label: High Flow
badges._note: Sourced from DealerInput boolean 'high_flow'. Registry feature_flags.high_flow_available is suppressed per locked CTL standard and must not be used as the badge trigger.

drop_cell_if.description: Drop cell if primary value is null or missing AND all fallback values are null or missing. Never render a blank label.
Drop cell if field_confidence[primary] (or the active fallback field) is below require_confidence. LOW always triggers drop. NONE bypasses check entirely.

spec_strip.render_hint: enum — capitalize first letter and render value directly: 'Vertical' or 'Radial'. This is the primary SSL-specific buyer filter. Vertical commands 10–20% resale premium. Drop cell only if value is null — never substitute a default.

spec_strip._note: Show high-flow GPM when present (not banned for SSL). Fall back to standard GPM if high-flow is absent. High-flow badge fires separately from badge_zone.

badges.label: High Flow
badges._note: Sourced from DealerInput boolean 'high_flow'. Registry feature_flags.high_flow_available is suppressed per locked SSL standard and must not be used as the badge trigger.

badges.label: Class D Specialty
badges._note: Composite badge. Fires when HP >= 85 AND high-flow GPM >= 36. Signals eligibility for forestry mulching, cold planing, and premium attachment market. Both conditions must pass with at least MEDIUM field_confidence to render.

drop_cell_if.description: Drop cell if primary value is null or missing AND all fallback values are null or missing. Never render a blank label.
Drop cell if field_confidence[primary] (or the active fallback field) is below require_confidence. LOW always triggers drop. NONE bypasses check entirely.

spec_strip._note: Primary class classifier for MEX. Weight bracket determines use-case eligibility before any other spec. Hard caps by class: A 0–4k lb, B 4–8k lb, C 8–13k lb, D 13–20k lb.
Core revenue spec. Scorer weight 30–40% for trenching and utility use cases. Hard gates: 5.0 ft utility, 7.0 ft septic, 8.0 ft deep trench, 9.5 ft sewer service.
Unit is feet. MEX registry stores this as max_dump_height_ft. CTL/SSL equivalent is dump_height_in (inches) — do not cross-reference or share rendering logic without unit conversion.

MEX canonical field is aux_flow_primary_gpm — not aux_flow_standard_gpm. hydraulic_flow_gpm removed from fallbacks: verified as a legacy duplicate with identical values across all 63 registry records, not a distinct concept. Pending deprecation — see MEX legacy field deprecation plan 2026-04-17.

badges.label: Zero Tail Swing
badges._note: tail_swing_type uses short value keys consistent across all equipment types: zero, conventional, reduced. Corrected 2026-04-17 — prior entry used 'zero_tail_swing' based on incorrect spec_display_profiles.json documentation; direct verification of all 63 MEX registry records confirmed short keys only. Zero tail swing unlocks interior demolition and tight-access work — hard cap rule in mini_ex_use_case_scorer.py lines 291–294.

drop_cell_if.description: Drop cell if primary value is null or missing AND all fallback values are null or missing. Never render a blank label.
Drop cell if field_confidence[primary] (or the active fallback field) is below require_confidence. LOW always triggers drop. NONE bypasses check entirely.
